claimBoundary: Budgets are declared before measurement. A metric gates deployment only when its mode is hard. Promoting a recorded metric to hard requires editing this file in a reviewed change; the performance receipt pins this file's SHA-256 so results can never quietly redefine their own thresholds.

default_lexical.description: The shipped default path: lexical compiler plus extended deterministic typo matching. No model may load or execute.

hybrid_optin.description: Opt-in cascade: lexical, then extended typo, then Qwen embedding for still-unresolved places only.

cascade.requirement: The receipt must report hit counts per tier. A probe resolved by an earlier tier must show zero executions in every later tier.

promotionNote: Recorded metrics gain hard maxima only after two receipts on unchanged budgets establish a baseline. The first hard set protects the invariants already known: the default lane stays model-free, deterministic hits never pay model cost, refusal and wrong-place guardrails carry over from the accuracy receipt, and no long task may stall the page outside the attributed load phase.
